cases.name: SHA-256 Hashing
cases.comment: Compute-heavy benchmark. Each measured transaction calls hash(index), which performs `index` rounds of a pure-Solidity SHA-256 (no inline assembly, no native sha256/keccak host function) and OR-folds the digests into the returned value so the optimiser cannot drop the work. Every iteration uses a fresh account so the compute-heavy transactions saturate the mempool in parallel; there are no cross-transaction dependencies, receipt waits, or captured variables.

inputs.comment: Allocating an account to use for deployment
Deploy the SHA-256 contract once, up front, using the conventional deployer (private key 1).
Measured loop: each iteration is one compute-heavy hashing transaction from its own fresh sender.

steps.comment: Fresh sender for this iteration so the transaction submits on its own nonce chain, in parallel with the others.
Perform 100 SHA-256 hashes in a single transaction.
